description: Golden test set for TrialMind evaluation — 50 queries with known ground truth

categories.sample_size: Questions about appropriate trial sample sizes and enrollment targets
categories.endpoint: Questions about primary endpoint selection and regulatory acceptability
categories.eligibility: Questions about inclusion/exclusion criteria and their impact on recruitment
categories.site: Questions about site selection, geography, and enrollment rates
categories.dropout: Questions about attrition rates and retention strategies
categories.failure: Questions about trial failure, termination, and root causes
categories.general: General benchmarking questions across multiple dimensions

test_cases.query: What was the typical sample size for Phase 3 trials in non-small cell lung cancer from 2015-2022?
test_cases.notes: Major Phase 3 NSCLC trials in immunotherapy era typically enrolled 500-1200 patients

test_cases.query: What is the median sample size for Phase 2 HER2-positive breast cancer trials?
test_cases.notes: Phase 2 HER2+ breast cancer trials often use 50-300 patients; single-arm ORR designs are common

test_cases.query: How many patients are typically enrolled in Phase 3 cardiovascular outcome trials (CVOTs)?
test_cases.notes: CVOTs are large trials; EMPA-REG OUTCOME enrolled 7020, LEADER enrolled 9340

test_cases.query: What enrollment rates per month are typical for Phase 3 rheumatoid arthritis trials?
test_cases.notes: RA trials have moderate enrollment rates due to established patient populations

test_cases.query: What sample sizes do Phase 2 single-arm oncology trials use for accelerated approval?
test_cases.notes: FDA accelerated approval single-arm trials typically need 50-200 patients with durable ORR

test_cases.query: What primary endpoints were used in FDA-approved Phase 3 PD-1 inhibitor trials for NSCLC?
test_cases.notes: KEYNOTE-189 used OS+PFS co-primary; KEYNOTE-024 used PFS; CheckMate 227 used OS+PFS

test_cases.query: What primary endpoints has FDA accepted for Phase 3 heart failure trials?
test_cases.notes: FDA accepts composite endpoints of CV death + HF hospitalization; also accepts KCCQ symptom scores

test_cases.query: Has pathological complete response been accepted as a primary endpoint for neoadjuvant breast cancer trials?
test_cases.notes: FDA accepted pCR for accelerated approval in neoadjuvant BC; requires confirmatory EFS data

test_cases.query: What surrogate endpoints are accepted in Phase 3 multiple myeloma trials?
test_cases.notes: PFS accepted; MRD negativity emerging as endpoint; OS historically required

test_cases.query: What are typical primary endpoints in Phase 3 diabetes clinical trials?
test_cases.notes: HbA1c reduction is standard; CVOTs required since 2008 FDA guidance

test_cases.query: What ECOG performance status requirements are most common in Phase 3 lung cancer trials?
test_cases.notes: PS 0-1 most common; some trials allow PS 0-2 for broader access; PS 2 exclusion affects enrollment

test_cases.query: What are common exclusion criteria in Phase 2 Alzheimer's disease trials?
test_cases.notes: Brain imaging requirements, cognitive score thresholds, and prior treatment exclusions common

test_cases.query: Are brain metastases typically excluded from Phase 3 NSCLC immunotherapy trials?
test_cases.notes: Many trials initially excluded; trend toward allowing stable treated brain metastases

test_cases.query: What PD-L1 expression cutoffs are most commonly used as inclusion criteria in NSCLC trials?
test_cases.notes: KEYNOTE-024 used PD-L1 TPS ≥50%; KEYNOTE-189 used PD-L1 ≥1% or all-comers

test_cases.query: What renal function requirements are typically used in Phase 3 oncology trials?
test_cases.notes: Typically eGFR ≥30-60 mL/min; varies by nephrotoxic drugs

test_cases.query: Which countries show the fastest enrollment rates for Phase 3 cardiovascular trials?
test_cases.notes: Eastern Europe often enrolls faster for CV trials; US has high costs but good patient access

test_cases.query: How many sites are typically activated in a Phase 3 oncology trial enrolling 600 patients?
test_cases.notes: Typically 80-150 sites for 600 patient Phase 3 oncology trial

test_cases.query: What is the typical enrollment rate per site per month in Phase 3 oncology trials?
test_cases.notes: Typically 0.5-2.0 patients/site/month in oncology Phase 3

test_cases.query: How many countries participate in a typical Phase 3 immunology clinical trial?
test_cases.notes: Large Phase 3 immunology trials often span 20-40 countries

test_cases.query: What dropout rates are typical in 24-month Phase 3 Alzheimer's trials?
test_cases.notes: Long-duration CNS trials have higher dropout; typically 20-40% in 24-month Alzheimer trials

test_cases.query: What completion rates are typical in Phase 3 rheumatoid arthritis trials with biologic treatment?
test_cases.notes: RA trials with biologics have moderate completion rates; adverse events and lack of efficacy are common reasons

test_cases.query: How does treatment-related adverse events impact dropout rates in Phase 1 oncology trials?
test_cases.notes: Phase 1 oncology trials have variable dropout due to safety; dose escalation affects retention

test_cases.query: What are the most common reasons Phase 3 oncology trials are terminated early?
test_cases.notes: Enrollment failure, safety signals, and interim futility are top reasons

test_cases.query: What percentage of Phase 3 clinical trials fail to meet their primary endpoint?
test_cases.notes: Approximately 40-50% of Phase 3 trials fail to meet primary endpoint

test_cases.query: What are common reasons for protocol amendments in Phase 2-3 oncology trials?
test_cases.notes: Eligibility criteria changes, dose modifications, and endpoint revisions are most common

test_cases.query: What design factors predict trial failure in neurodegenerative disease?
test_cases.notes: Poor endpoint sensitivity, wrong biomarker selection, and late-stage patient populations common

test_cases.query: How long do Phase 3 cardiovascular trials typically take from first patient enrolled to primary completion?
test_cases.notes: Large Phase 3 CV trials typically take 3-6 years from FPI to primary completion

test_cases.query: What percentage of Phase 3 oncology trials achieve their planned enrollment target?
test_cases.notes: Studies suggest 30-40% of trials fail to meet enrollment targets on time

test_cases.query: What is the typical trial duration for a Phase 2 immuno-oncology trial?
test_cases.notes: Phase 2 IO trials typically run 18-36 months depending on endpoint
